prompt: Complete using the correct form of be going to and the verbs in brackets. You may have to use some negative forms.
explanation: Intentions and evidence-based predictions use be going to. Questions invert the form of be (Is Lauren going to tell...?); negatives insert not before going to (isn't going to invite).
passageTemplate:
1. When I grow up, I {{blank-1}} (play) guitar in a rock group!
2. Rick and Mark {{blank-2}} (start) going to the gym twice a week.
3. {{blank-3}} Lauren {{blank-4}} (tell) her mum about what happened?
4. I {{blank-5}} (look) on the Internet for information about snowboarding.
5. No, Nadine {{blank-6}} (invite) everyone from class – just her close friends.
6. {{blank-7}} Harry {{blank-8}} (be) ready on time or not?
7. Careful! You {{blank-9}} (break) something with that ball! Go outside!
8. I {{blank-10}} (lie down) for half an hour. Call me at six o'clock.
